classes.reasoning: This document presents efficacy and safety results for a completed randomised controlled trial, including patient disposition, primary endpoint analysis (CDR-SB at Week 78), key secondary endpoints, biomarker data, and detailed safety reporting. The structure and content match a Clinical Study Report section presenting final trial results.

summary: This is a section of a Clinical Study Report presenting efficacy and safety results for a phase 2/3 trial of velagostat versus placebo in patients with early Alzheimer's disease. The document reports that 477 patients received treatment, the primary endpoint (CDR-SB at Week 78) was met with statistical significance (p<0.001), and all key secondary endpoints including cognitive and functional measures plus tau PET imaging were also met. Safety profile was comparable to placebo with no treatment-related deaths.
content:
...continued from previous page


4. EFFICACY RESULTS

4.1  Patient Disposition and Analysis Populations

Of 480 patients randomised, 477 received at least one dose of study treatment
(3 patients withdrew consent before first dose). The mITT population comprised
477 patients (320 velagostat, 157 placebo). The PP population comprised 431
patients; 46 excluded due to protocol deviations, most commonly missed cognitive
assessments outside the protocol window (n=31).

  Disposition              Velagostat (n=320)   Placebo (n=160)
  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  Completed 96 weeks         241 (75.3%)          119 (74.4%)
  Discontinued                79 (24.7%)           41 (25.6%)
    AE                         22  (6.9%)           11  (6.9%)
    Withdrawal                 28  (8.8%)           16 (10.0%)
    Lost to follow-up          14  (4.4%)            8  (5.0%)
    Death                      15  (4.7%)            6  (3.8%)

  Deaths on study: 21 total. All reviewed by independent adjudication
  committee. No deaths considered treatment-related.

4.2  Primary Endpoint -- CDR-SB at Week 78

  Timepoint    Velagostat  Placebo   LS Mean Diff   95% CI          p-value
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Week 13       +0.19       +0.38     -0.19         (-0.44, +0.06)   0.14
  Week 26       +0.43       +0.77     -0.34         (-0.65, -0.03)   0.032
  Week 52       +0.82       +1.39     -0.57         (-0.93, -0.21)   0.002
  Week 78       +1.14       +1.87     -0.73         (-1.12, -0.34)  <0.001
  Week 96       +1.31       +2.14     -0.83         (-1.25, -0.41)  <0.001

  PRIMARY ENDPOINT MET: -0.73 points at Week 78 (p<0.001), exceeding the
  pre-specified minimal clinically important difference of >=0.5 points.

4.3  Key Secondary Endpoints at Week 78

  ADAS-Cog13:    +2.4 vs +4.1  -- difference -1.7 points (p=0.003)
  ADCS-ADL-MCI:  -3.2 vs -5.8  -- difference +2.6 points (p=0.001)
  MMSE:          -1.8 vs -3.1  -- difference +1.3 points (p=0.012)
  Amyloid PET:   -2.9 vs -1.6  -- NS (p=0.44) -- confirms non-amyloid MOA
  Tau PET SUVR (entorhinal): -26.4% vs -3.8% (p<0.001)
  Tau PET SUVR (temporal):   -23.1% vs -3.2% (p<0.001)

  All key secondary endpoints met statistical significance.

4.4  Exploratory Biomarkers

  Plasma p-tau 181 (Week 78): -38.4% (velagostat) vs -2.1% (placebo), p<0.001
  Plasma NfL (Week 78):       +4.2% vs +18.7% (p=0.002) -- attenuation of
                               neurodegeneration marker increase
  APOE e4 subgroup (n=284):   LS mean diff -0.81 (p<0.001)
  Non-e4 subgroup (n=193):    LS mean diff -0.59 (p=0.018)
  No significant treatment-by-genotype interaction (p=0.31).

  CSF sub-study (n=60, paired baseline/Week 78):
    p-tau 181: -41.2% vs -3.4% (p<0.001)
    Total tau:  -28.7% vs -1.8% (p<0.001)
    Strongest evidence of central tau target engagement.

4.5  Time to Confirmed Cognitive Decline

  Pre-specified exploratory endpoint (CDR-SB increase >=1 point, confirmed):
    Velagostat: median 62.4 weeks (95% CI 54.1-NR)
    Placebo:    median 44.8 weeks (95% CI 38.2-54.6)
    HR 0.64 (95% CI 0.49-0.84), p=0.001 -- 36% risk reduction

5. SAFETY RESULTS

5.1  Overview

  Parameter                    Velagostat (n=320)   Placebo (n=160)
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Any TEAE                      91.3%                88.8%
  Grade >=3 TEAE                18.4%                17.5%
  SAE                           14.1%                13.1%
  Discontinuation due to AE      6.9%                 6.9%
  Deaths (all cause)             4.7%                 3.8%
  Deaths (treatment-related)     0%                   0%

5.2  TEAEs of Special Interest

  Falls (any):             22.2% vs 26.9%  -- no excess vs placebo
  Headache:                26.6% vs 18.1%  -- mild; Grade 3: 0%
  Nausea:                  20.9% vs 13.8%  -- mild; resolves Week 8
  Dizziness:               17.8% vs 9.4%   -- mild; no syncope
  ALT >3x ULN:              1.9% vs 0.6%
  ARIA-E (MRI):             0%   vs 0%
  ARIA-H (MRI):             0.3% vs 0%  -- 1 asymptomatic microbleed

...continues on next page (Section 6: Pharmacokinetics)
